name: The Strauss Family's Tipsy Squire Pudding
recipeInstructions:
1. Line a bowl with ladyfingers on the bottom and sides in a single layer and set aside. Reserve the remaining ladyfingers.
2. In a medium-size bowl, soak almonds and raisins in 2 tablespoons of bourbon and set aside.
3. Scald milk (heat until bubbles form at edges) and set aside.
4. Combine sugar, cornstarch, flour, and salt in the top of a double boiler over simmering water on medium-high heat; whisk in scalded milk, stirring well.
5. Reduce heat, cover pan, and cook for 15 minutes, stirring often.
6. Beat egg yolks and egg whites in separate bowls with clean beaters.
7. Remove pan from heat and pour a little of the milk mixture over the egg yolks. Mix well then return egg mixture to pan.
8. Cook for 2 minutes longer, whisking constantly until smooth. Whisk in vanilla and remaining 1/2 cup of bourbon.
9. Fold in beaten egg whites. Pour one-third of the custard over ladyfingers in bowl.
10. Sprinkle with one-third of the almond/raisin mixture, then add a layer of lady fingers on top.
11. Repeat layers until bowl is almost full, finishing with a layer of lady fingers.
12. Garnish as desired.
